cards.title: Supporting Sensitive Teens Through Social Media Pressure
cards.body: Adolescents who feel different from peers can struggle with self-worth when comparing themselves on social media. As they mature, their world naturally expands to include more like-minded people who validate their uniqueness.

cards.title: College Tours: Letting Kids Lead Decision-Making
cards.body: Parents can support their teenagers' college choices by taking an active but secondary role during campus visits. The key is balancing parental guidance with allowing young adults to drive their own decision-making process.

cards.title: When One Adult Child Drains Family Resources
cards.body: Parents who focus heavily on supporting a struggling child may inadvertently neglect their other children's emotional needs. The sibling who seemed independent may be silently paying a significant emotional price for this imbalance.

cards.title: Understanding Rising ADHD Diagnosis Rates in Children
cards.body: ADHD diagnosis rates have increased, but this may reflect better awareness and access to services rather than a true epidemic. More families are seeking evaluations as recognition of symptoms improves across schools and communities.

cards.title: Helping Children Understand Grief and Loss
cards.body: Teaching children about death and grief through real experiences helps them develop healthy emotional resilience. Shielding young people from mortality may delay crucial psychological development until adulthood.

cards.title: Young Crocodile Attack Survivor Makes Remarkable Recovery
cards.body: A three-year-old who was attacked by a zoo crocodile has undergone seven operations and is now smiling again. The child's parents report positive progress in both physical healing and emotional recovery.

cards.title: Managing Family Money: Addressing Unequal Financial Burden
cards.body: When one partner takes full responsibility for household finances, resentment and imbalance can develop over time. Couples should discuss money management openly to ensure both partners feel heard and valued in financial decisions.
